response: The internet’s history traces back to 1960s U.S. Department of Defense–funded networking research, with ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969 when a message was sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. It expanded through the 1970s, then in 1983 the adoption of TCP/IP protocols laid the foundation for the modern internet. In 1989, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN, and the first widely accessible browser, Mosaic, launched in 1993; subsequent developments included the late-1990s dot-com boom (many firms failed in the 2001 crash) and survivors like Amazon and Google. Social media (mid-2000s), mobile internet after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing (AWS in 2006) drove further growth, and today the internet connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly all areas of modern life.
